documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone -b stable https://github.com/br101/horst; git clone --recursive https://github.com/br101/horst. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `horst -i wlan0 -N -q`. Further invocations from upstream: `horst -n IP`. HORST - Highly Optimized Radio Scanning Tool or "Horsts OLSR Radio Scanning Tool".
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: HORST - Highly Optimized Radio Scanning Tool
or "Horsts OLSR Radio Scanning Tool".
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: HORST - Highly Optimized Radio Scanning Tool or "Horsts OLSR Radio Scanning Tool".

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Wireless, use horst because HORST - Highly Optimized Radio Scanning Tool
or "Horsts OLSR Radio Scanning Tool".
When you need a concrete invocation, run `horst -i wlan0 -N -q` as documented upstream for horst.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `horst -n IP` as documented upstream for horst.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair horst with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `horst --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
